data.title: Wells Fargo CIO raises S&P 500 target, advises against small caps
data.description:
Darrell Cronk, chief investment officer for wealth and investment management at Wells Fargo, raised his year-end S&P 500 (SP500) target to between 6,600 and 6,800 amid new market record highs and the Federal Reserve’s easing cycle.

Cronk shared his market outlook during an interview with CNBC, noting that while he expects volatility to increase, the current economic conditions are favorable for continued growth.

The Federal Reserve’s recent interest rate cuts have created a positive market environment, according to Cronk. “High yield spreads are at fresh lows this morning,” he said, adding that banks (KBE), (KRE) are at their all-time highs despite the rate cuts – a rare occurrence that historically suggests economic strength rather than weakness.

He also was particularly optimistic about long-term market prospects. “2026 probably sets up to be an even better year,” he said, citing stable fiscal policies, continued monetary easing, and strong corporate balance sheets.

He emphasized that “the markets are telling you, ‘Hey, things are a go for the remainder of this year and into next year.’”

When discussing specific market segments, Cronk expressed skepticism about small caps (IWM) despite their recent outperformance. He acknowledged that small caps typically perform well in two scenarios: emerging from recessions and during aggressive Fed cutting cycles. However, he questioned the sustainability of their current strength, pointing to significant market cap disparities.

Cronk specifically recommended against rotating investments from technology (XLK) to small caps (IWM). “This whole idea that you’re going to rotate from tech to small cap…is, to us, a little bit nonsensical.”

He cited concerns about quality deterioration in the small cap universe, noting that “private capital’s come in there and cherry-picked a lot of the good companies and taken them back private.”

As a result of these concerns, Cronk confirmed that Wells Fargo recently moved to an underweight position on small caps (IWM), (SP600). “And we think that’s still the right trade to hold.”

data.title: Wynn Resorts is tipped by Stifel to stay in rally mode
data.description:
Stifel raised estimates on Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) on its view that the casino company has multiple catalysts in play.

Analyst Steven Wieczynski pointed to recent healthier trends emerging out of the Macau gaming market, including easier access into the market through improved visa schemes and further government stimulus. Macau GGR is seen rising for both the mass and VIP segments.

Despite the recent rally in Wynn (NASDAQ:WYNN) shares, Wieczynski said valuation remains underwhelming and continues to discount the recovery in Macau. "We think there is considerable upside left in WYNN and continue to believe this name should remain a core holding for any investor," wrote Wieczynski. Stifel boosted its price target moves on Wynn (WYNN) to $145 off higher 2026-2027 EBITDA estimates.

Shares of Wynn Resorts (WYNN) were up 2.8% in Thursday afternoon trading and are 17% higher over the last six weeks. Wall Street analysts have a consensus Strong Buy rating on WYNN, while Seeking Alpha analysts are down a notch at Buy.

data.title: Onex to buy Integrated Specialty Coverage from KKR
data.description:
Onex Corporation (TSX:ONEX:CA) said on Thursday that its Onex Partners Opportunity Fund agreed to acquire a tech-enabled insurance platform, Integrated Specialty Coverages ("ISC"), from KKR (NYSE:KKR). 

The investment by Onex Partners is being made alongside the Public Sector Pension Investment Board (“PSP Investments”), Ardian (through its Co-Investment strategy), and several other institutional equity partners. ISC’s management team and employees will remain significant shareholders following the transaction.

The insurance sector has been a core focus for Onex Partners.  "This investment continues Onex Partners’ long history of successfully investing across the entire property & casualty insurance value chain, particularly in founder-led businesses," the company said.

Onex (TSX:ONEX:CA) stock rose 0.8% in afternoon trading in Toronto.

Financial terms of the deal weren't disclosed. The transaction is expected to close later this year.

data.title: Coinbase launches USDC onchain lending product yielding as much as 10.8%
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) is introducing an onchain lending product that lets users earn yields of up to 10.8% on their USDC (USDC-USD) stablecoin deposits, it said on Thursday.

The new feature will be rolled out to users over the coming weeks in the U.S. (excluding NY State), Bermuda, and additional countries, the crypto exchange said in a blog post.

When a user deposit its USDC (USDC-USD), Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) will create a smart contract wallet to connect to the Morpho protocol through onchain vaults managed by Steakhouse Financial, where the funds will be allocated across various lending markets. Funds are always accessible and can be withdrawn at any time when liquidity permits.

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) already offers users passive USDC (USDC-USD) rewards of 4.1% annual percentage rate, or 4.5% for Coinbase One members, it said.

USDC (USDC-USD) is the dollar-backed stablecoin issued by Circle Internet Group (CRCL).

data.title: Immunocore initiated at neutral at Guggenheim as Kimmtrak priced into shares
data.description:
• Guggenheim has initiated Immunocore Holdings (NASDAQ:IMCR) with a neutral rating saying that while the company has a "solid commercial foundation" with Kimmtrak (tebentafusp) for metastatic uveal melanoma, the value of the drug is already priced into the stock. 
  • The firm does not have a price target.
  • Analyst Paul Jeng noted that "we are seeing fewer sources of commercial outperformance for Kimmtrak as US mUM market penetration continues to plateau, contributions from new ex-US launches taper, and real-world duration of therapy is fully appreciated."
  • He added that Kimmtrak could face competitive pressure from a combination treatment of Ideaya Biosciences (IDYA) darovasertib and Pfizer's (PFE) Xalkori (crizotinib). The combo will have regulatory and data catalysts in 2026 "that may serve as negative lateral catalysts on potential for the regimen to erode Kimmtrak’s market share in HLA-A2+ patients."

data.title: Nvidia, Intel plan to rely on TSMC in fabricating 'revolutionary' chips for data center, PCs
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) will provide the foundry support for the "revolutionary" chips they are making with Intel (NASDAQ:INTC).

"Nvidia and Intel are both successful customers of TSMC," Huang said during a press conference on Thursday afternoon. "They are a world-class foundry and support customers of diverse needs. You can't overstate the magic that is TSMC. But today, our partnership is 100% focused on the custom CPUs we are building for data centers that can connect to the Nvidia AI ecosystem."

"We both still have a lot of respect for TSMC, and we will continue to work with them," said Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan. "We will still work on 14a and 18a and see if that can be used at some point in the future."

As part of the deal, which featured a $5B investment by Nvidia in Intel, the two companies will use Nvidia's NVLink, bringing Nvidia's artificial intelligence and accelerated computing strength and Intel's x86 architecture. They plan to combine Intel CPUs with Nvidia GPUs to create products for data center and PC markets.

"Together, our companies will create custom CPUs for data center platforms," Huang said. "For personal computing, we are going to create new Nvidia chips that integrate and fuse the world's best CPUs and GPUs. The era of accelerated and AI computing has arrived. Intel and Nvidia are partnered to drive it forward."

"This corroboration will build on the core strengths of both companies," Tan said. "This will unleash the new era of x86 innovation. I'm excited about what we can create together. Clearly, this is all about the scale. We can do even more working together ... Right now we are focused on collaboration, and we can talk about the process down the road."

Huang said the new products will have a total addressable market of more than $50B when considering their utilization in data centers and PCs.

"There is an entire segment of the market that ties in CPU and GPU that Nvidia has largely not addressed," Huang said. "We use NVLink to fuse the CPU and GPU into a new class of integrated laptops the market has not seen yet ... This is going to address some $50B per year opportunity ... The data center CPU market is $25B and the PC notebook market is 150M sold per year."

Huang also said the deal will not affect its relationship with Arm (ARM).

"We will continue to build our Arm roadmap, and this won't affect that at all," he said.

He said the deal was created solely by Huang and Tan.

"The Trump Administration had no involvement in this partnership at all," Huang said. "They would have been supportive, of course. I told (U.S. Commerce) Secretary (Howard) Lutnick today, and he was very excited about two American companies working together."

Analysts believe the partnership provides upside for Intel, but the company still needs to show more that it's back as a chipmaking power.

"This investment in INTC represents a large vote of confidence from NVDA," said Julian Lin, Investing Group Leader for Best of Breed Growth Stocks. "The momentum may go a long way towards the company's turnaround, but I emphasize that it still needs to show more signs of progress towards that turnaround. In comparison to other stories like Oracle (ORCL), this latest development appears to have more hopes built into the momentum."

"A bit of an alliance between NVDA and INTC should be a net negative for AMD (AMD), as it means more powerful competition, I believe," added Jonathan Weber, Investing Group Leader for Cash Flow Club. "TSM may lose some foundry business to Intel in the future but remains very well positioned and the clear manufacturing leader for the time being."

Arm shares had slipped 3.5% during afternoon trading, while TSM was up 2%. AMD had declined 1.5%.

data.title: FactSet outlines $100M–$150M organic ASV growth target as AI investments accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: FactSet Research Systems Inc. (FDS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Sanoke Viswanathan addressed investors as the new CEO, emphasizing FactSet's strategic inflection point and focus on "AI and data-driven innovation" to transform financial services. He noted, "We have a unique opportunity to become the leading AI-powered financial intelligence platform for our clients." Viswanathan highlighted FactSet’s open platform, client-centric culture, and deep workflow integration as competitive advantages.
  • Viswanathan reported reviews of the product road map, client implementations, and AI initiatives, stating, "My focus will be on engaging with clients on their top priorities and continuing to build my knowledge base about FactSet's data, tools and services."
  • Helen Shan, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "For FY '25, we added $127 million of organic ASV, which is near the top end of our guidance range. Annual revenue increased to $2.3 billion, while adjusted operating margin was 36.3% and adjusted EPS grew to $16.98, all comfortably within our guidance ranges." She highlighted progress on FactSet’s AI road map, expansion of the open platform, and product integration, including LiquidityBook and Irwin acquisitions.
  • Shan noted that Q4 organic ASV of $81.8 million was the largest in company history. She described regional and firm-type performance, with strong results in wealth, asset management, and analytics, and pointed to significant growth in Asia Pacific and EMEA.

Outlook

  • Management guided for fiscal 2026 organic ASV growth of $100 million to $150 million, representing approximately 5% growth at the midpoint. Shan explained, "We are taking a conservative approach to our guidance to reflect the current environment of longer sales cycles and more rigorous client approval processes and not due to reduced confidence in our competitive positioning or market demand."
  • GAAP revenues for 2026 are expected to be between $2.42 billion and $2.45 billion, with a GAAP operating margin range of 29.5% to 31% and adjusted operating margin of 34% to 35.5%.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance for 2026 is $16.90 to $17.60. Net interest expense is expected between $43 million to $48 million, with capital expenditures projected at $110 million to $120 million.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenues increased 6.2% year-over-year, reaching $597 million. FactSet delivered 5.4% overall revenue growth and 4.4% organic growth for fiscal 2025.
  • Q4 adjusted operating margin was 33.8%. Adjusted EPS for the quarter rose 8% year-over-year to $4.05.
  • Headcount grew less than 2% in the quarter, primarily in low-cost locations. People-related expenses increased $27 million or 13%, technology expenses rose $8 million or 13%, and third-party content costs increased $3 million. FactSet managed other expenses through improved receivables collection and reduced discretionary spend.
  • The company returned over $460 million to shareholders in fiscal 2025 through dividends and share repurchases. They ended the year with a gross debt leverage ratio of 1.5x, and divested RMS Partners, a noncore sell-side research platform, resulting in a onetime gain recognized in GAAP results.

Q&A

  • Alex Kramm, UBS: Asked about margin decline and investment impact. Shan explained, "We're investing about, let's call it, 250 basis points really in growth investments, 2/3 of it will be in growth investments and 1/3 a bit more structural."
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank: Inquired about AI-driven competitive dynamics and longer-term focus. Viswanathan responded, "My focus in the next several weeks and months is going to be spending significant time with our clients to understand their top priorities... and looking at our road map and understanding how we are meeting our client demand."
  • Ashish Sabadra, RBC: Asked about wealth segment growth. Skoko highlighted expansion deals and geographic opportunity, stating, "We had 2 7-figure expansion deals in the quarter... wealth will continue to be our growth driver for years to come."
  • Kelsey Zhu, Autonomous Research: Sought details on AI strategy and ASV growth from GenAI. Shan said, "When we gave the guidance of 30 to 50 basis points, I'm really happy to say that we were exactly in the middle."
  • Shlomo Rosenbaum, Stifel: Asked about AI investments and growth acceleration. Viswanathan stated, "We are still in a relatively early phase of embedding of AI in our clients' workflows... there is a breakout phase that is well ahead of us."
  • Jason Haas, Wells Fargo: Asked about bank hiring trends. Skoko reported, "Hiring in Q4 was a bit better than expected for us."
  • Surinder Thind, Jefferies: Asked about AI and productivity impact on costs. Shan said, "You'll see a slowdown in employee growth but a lot of our improvements will be about redeploying that talent and then we're going to see that translate into greater productivity and higher output."
  • Other analysts raised questions about margin trajectory, payback periods, pricing, capital allocation, and AI product development.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on margin pressure, AI investments, and competitive dynamics, showing a slightly skeptical but constructive tone.
  • Management’s tone in prepared remarks was confident, focusing on execution and growth opportunities. During Q&A, responses were measured, with Shan often providing detailed breakdowns and Viswanathan emphasizing ongoing learning and client engagement.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a confident stance but showed increased caution in guidance, and analysts were more focused on the impact of investments and margin dynamics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from reaffirming confidence in FY25 to a more conservative stance for FY26, citing longer sales cycles and rigorous client approval processes.
  • Management focus moved from transition planning (previous CEO) to articulating AI strategy and platform integration (new CEO).
  • Analysts increased focus on AI impact, expense management, and margin trajectory. Management provided more granular detail on investment allocation and productivity expectations.
  • Key metrics improved, with record Q4 organic ASV and higher year-over-year revenue growth. Adjusted operating margin decreased from the previous quarter.
  • Confidence in AI initiatives and product adoption was more explicitly detailed this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tight client budgets, evolving market dynamics, and longer sales cycles as ongoing challenges.
  • Pricing discipline and competitive headwinds in certain regions and segments were addressed, with management noting stabilization in new business discounting.
  • Shan discussed the risk of increased technology and content costs, emphasizing "cost discipline" and the need to balance investments with productivity gains.
  • Analyst concerns included margin pressure, payback periods on investments, and timing of AI-driven growth.

Final Takeaway

FactSet management emphasized that the company is at a strategic inflection point, with AI investments fueling record organic ASV growth and strong revenue momentum. The leadership transition has brought renewed focus on client engagement and AI-powered innovation, while fiscal 2026 guidance reflects a conservative approach amid evolving market dynamics and longer sales cycles. Management remains confident that disciplined investments and platform enhancements position FactSet for continued growth and long-term shareholder value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.K. Leonardo workers to vote on strike action after pay dispute
data.description:
Roughly 3,000 U.K. employees of Italian defense company Leonardo (OTCPK:FINMF) (OTCPK:FINMY) are preparing to ballot for strike action after rejecting a recent pay offer, trade union Unite said Thursday.

The vote, covering staff at sites in Yeovil, Edinburgh, Newcastle, Luton,and Basildon, will remain open until early October, according to the union.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court to hear tariff case on Nov. 5
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court will hear arguments regarding the legality of President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs on Nov. 5, according to the court's website. 

Last month, an appeals court ruled that most of Trump's tariffs are illegal, saying that the president had exceeded his authority in his use of emergency powers to ratchet up tariffs against most of U.S.'s trading partners. Trump used the 1977 law known as the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, which had never before been used to justify placing tariffs on imports, to impose the so-called "Liberation Day" tariffs.

Earlier this month, the U.S.'s highest court said it would take up the case and schedule the hearing for sometime in November. A group of small businesses and 12 U.S. states sued the administration, claiming the way the tariffs were imposed are illegal.

The Supreme Court will hear arguments on two related lawsuits — Learning Resources Inc. v. Donald J. Trump, President of the United States, et al. and Donald J. Trump, President of the United States, et al., petitioners v. V.O.S. Selections Inc., et al.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Plug Power set for seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Shares of Plug Power (NASDAQ:PLUG) were on track for their seventh consecutive session of gains on Thursday, as the stock rose more than 4% to $2.09 in afternoon trade.

The company gained nearly 39% in the past six trading sessions. The stock closed nearly 19% higher, at $2 on Wednesday. PLUG is up nearly 26% over the past one month.

Seeking Alpha author Henrix Alex highlighted that rare insider purchases and the extension/reinstatement of tax credits have caused shares to rally in recent months.

“On the conference call, management guided full-year revenues below consensus expectations but reiterated expectations for gross margins to reach break-even levels in the fourth quarter,” noted Alex.

Alex rated the stock as Sell, stating that weak order intake and high cash usage continue to threaten Plug Power's viability.

Seeking Alpha analysts also rated the stock as Sell.

As per the Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, PLUG has a Hold rating with a score of 2.91 out of 5. The company received B+ for momentum and B for valuation, but an F grade in profitability and C- in revisions dragged down the rating.

The stock has declined over 6% so far this year, compared to over 12% gain in the broader benchmark index.

Turning to the Wall Street community, six out of 22 analysts gave BA a Buy and above rating and 12 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while four recommended Strong Sell.
